text: ## Why watermark your photos

- Professional photographers sharing portfolio images online before client deliv
- Stock photographers who need to display preview versions of images for sale
- Content creators and bloggers who publish original photography with their work
- Businesses and agencies protecting proprietary product images or marketing mat
- Social media creators building a visual brand across platforms

## The batch watermarking workflow
Watermarking individual photos in a photo editor is fine for a handful of images
The standard batch watermarking workflow has four steps: prepare your watermark
### What makes a good watermark asset
- For logos: a PNG file with transparent background, at least 400px wide for hig

- For QR codes: generate at a minimum of 300x300px so it remains scannable when
- Use white or light-colored elements so the watermark is visible on both light
- Keep the watermark proportionally small- between 8% and 15% of the image width

### Placement

- Center placement at 30–40% opacity is harder to crop out and more effective as
- Avoid pure white space in corners- a watermark placed over a white sky or blan
- Tiled watermarks (the watermark repeated across the entire image as a diagonal
### Opacity

### Size

Not everyone agrees that watermarks are worth it. A vocal segment of the photogr
### The case against watermarks

- Heavy watermarks reduce the perceived quality of your portfolio and may cost y
- For photographers who rely on shareability- travel bloggers, social media crea

### The case for watermarks
- The majority of image theft is opportunistic, not deliberate. A visible waterm
- A watermark provides clear evidence of authorship in any dispute or DMCA taked
- For stock photographers and commercial shooters, a watermarked preview standar
- Even a removed watermark leaves visible evidence of tampering in some tools, w
